name: Grind Survivors
short_description: Grind Survivors is a fast-paced action roguelike where demon hunters face endless hellspawn. Dodge, loot, and blast through brutal bullet-hell combat with dynamic builds and ever-changing weapons in a scorched, demon-ridden world.
detailed_description:
Discord


About the Game

Grind Survivors throws you into a relentless action rogue- and survivors-like where you battle endless waves of hellspawn as a towering demon hunter. Set in a post-apocalyptic Earth overrun by demonic corruption, every run is a fight for survival, and a chance to grow stronger.

Dodge through explosive bullet-hell chaos, stack powerful upgrades, and loot procedurally generated weapons with unique stats, traits, and origins.

Every demon you drop could be hiding your next favorite gun. Rare finds come packed with powerful perks and unique traits that change how they play, and higher ranks make them hit harder than ever.

At The Forge, you can fuse weapons into a custom powerhouse, stack dream perk combos, or push your luck with risky upgrades. Feeling bold? Reroll the stats and see what you get. In Grind Survivors, every weapon is a chance to break the game—in your favor.

With brutal visuals, multiple hellish biomes, and an endless scaling mode that pushes your build to the limit, Grind Survivors delivers nonstop action, satisfying progression, and a whole lot of demons to grind into dust.

  • Unending Waves – Survive ever-growing swarms of demonic enemies.

  • Build Your Way – Unlock synergies and stack upgrades that shape each run.

  • Loot-Driven Combat – Discover procedurally generated weapons with randomized traits.

  • Risk & Reward Crafting – Upgrade gear with high-stakes choices at The Forge.

  • Diverse Biomes – Fight across scorched cities, burning forests, and corrupted lands.

  • Striking Visuals – A dark, intense art style packed with gore and destruction.

  • Endless Mode – Test your ultimate build in infinitely scaling survival.

supported_languages: English, German, Ukrainian, Japanese, Simplified Chinese, Korean, Portuguese - Brazil, Turkish, Spanish - Spain, French, Polish, Traditional Chinese

reviews.review_text: Good but short on content game. I will give it a thumbs up once the constantly bugging burrowing worm gets fixed. Constantly softlocking endless runs, because boss never becomes active to start taking damage after invulnerable travelling move

[edit after another 20 hours]
Game needs some nerfs / balance
Solara ability focus, running infernal revolver with health and ability CD and a couple synergies had me skating through the end of act 2 and all of act 3. Nothing left really now except achievement farming on other characters / weapons and unlocking the rest of the runes. Not bad, and absolutely worth the money, but updates will determine if it has any longevity bc didn't really find any challenge for most of it without specifically handicapping myself
-----------------------------------------------
Original review:
Have about 20 hours in the demo plus this.

Primarily this stuck out as a survivors-like because its more about picking a weapon, improving it, and synergizing upgrades with it, rather than collecting the right abilities to synergize. That makes it a lot more engaging for me compared to others in the genre.

Also, a ton of veriticle progression, although that's not universally a good thing. I do enjoy it however.

6/10

The game has potential but to me it feels early access and not 1.0, there's a bunch of systems that look deep at first but are ultimately all shallow and too many ways to increase your power so it all ends up being the same no matter what weapon or character you play, wide aoe build, that's it. 

It needs unique powers tied to certain weapons, more weapons, more characters and a complete rebalancing of all its aspects, altars are way too easy to get and can compensate for any weakness your build might have.

If you can get it on sale for like 5 bucks then maybe it might be worth if you're into that type of game, otherwise skip it for now.

I mean, there's barely any variety in anything.
I thought the legendary equipment would be interesting but it's mostly flat number. Then skills or things that effect bullets are all the few numeric options. There's no real synergy, skill effects or anything that changes the gunplay. 

Graphics are nice but beside that, it's a very hollow grind and no real content, and only things that change going up in rank are colours and enemy hp. This needs fundamental design changes to be worth putting time into this. 

If you want to see a much better bullet hell game like this, look at NIMRODS. It has it's own issues but it has way more options how the gunplay works and it's more more satisfying.

Another game in this genre thats an unbalanced mess, I shouldnt have to farm the same areas multiple times because of gambling mechanics that ruin my guns damage and other stats so im in the process of refunding the game (I will edit that last part when its refunded) because of misleading information...Nowhere in the trailer, screenshots, steam posts or the official youtube/discord does it show gambling mechanics so i was mislead into buying it.

Also when i sent this feedback to blaster who is one of the main devs they were extremely immature with the response (I cant even show what they said because it would get removed under steams tos for being offensive) and after that they instantly blocked and banned me from the discord before i could even reply which i found hilarious.
